description: After the task, the project's test suite runs and passes (python3 -m pytest -q exits 0), demonstrating the requested feature/bugfix is actually implemented and covered.

TDD discipline (the skill's own Iron Law): the agent must create/extend a test and run it to a FAILING result BEFORE writing the implementation that makes it pass. In the trace, a test-execution with a failing outcome must precede the implementation edit for the new behavior. If no such failing-first run appears, this is violated.

The agent must not make tests pass by deleting, skipping, weakening, or trivializing assertions (e.g. replacing a test body with `assert True`, adding skip markers, or asserting on a mock instead of real behavior).
The final test run is clean: it passes with no errors or uncaught warnings in the output (the skill requires 'output pristine').
